meaning: adj.涓嶆敞鎰忕殑,涓嶉伒瀹堢殑
examples: A good student of science can't be an unobservant person.
The careless, the slow, the unobservant, the lazy fail to see it, or clutch at it when it has gone.
An unobservant zookeeper is followed home by all the animals he thinks he has left behind in the zoo.
Shanghai remained unobservant of its Jewish legacy for three decades.
